text.content: "You made graphs with scale of 5. Let's connect what you learned to the world around you."

rich_text.plain_text: "You made graphs with scale of 5. Let's connect what you learned to the world around you."

text.content: 1.1  Real World Bridge Type C  [s1_1_real_world_bridge_type_c]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.1  Real World Bridge Type C  [s1_1_real_world_bridge_type_c]

text.content: Four real-world images displayed: a hand showing 5 fingers, tally marks grouped by 5s, nickels worth 5 cents each, and a clock face with marks every 5 minutes.

rich_text.plain_text: Four real-world images displayed: a hand showing 5 fingers, tally marks grouped by 5s, nickels worth 5 cents each, and a clock face with marks every 5 minutes.

text.content: "You've been grouping by 5s all lesson. But 5s show up everywhere in real life. Which of these uses groups of 5?"

rich_text.plain_text: "You've been grouping by 5s all lesson. But 5s show up everywhere in real life. Which of these uses groups of 5?"

text.content:
tool: multi_select  options: ["Hand", "Tally marks", "Nickels", "Clock"]
"Which of these uses groups of 5? Select ALL that apply."

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multi_select  options: ["Hand", "Tally marks", "Nickels", "Clock"]
"Which of these uses groups of 5? Select ALL that apply."

text.content: ✅ Student selected all four options, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected all four options, correct

text.content: "Right. All of them! Hands have 5 fingers. Tallies group by 5s. Nickels are worth 5 cents. Clocks mark every 5 minutes. Grouping by 5s is everywhere, and now you use it for graphs too."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. All of them! Hands have 5 fingers. Tallies group by 5s. Nickels are worth 5 cents. Clocks mark every 5 minutes. Grouping by 5s is everywhere, and now you use it for graphs too."

text.content: ❌ Student has not selected all correct answers (first attempt)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student has not selected all correct answers (first attempt)

text.content: "Look at each image carefully. Do you see groups of 5 in the picture?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Look at each image carefully. Do you see groups of 5 in the picture?"

text.content: ❌ Student has not selected all correct answers (second attempt)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student has not selected all correct answers (second attempt)

text.content: All four images pulse to draw attention.

rich_text.plain_text: All four images pulse to draw attention.

text.content: "Not quite. Count the fingers on a hand. Look at how tally marks group. Check what a nickel is worth. Count the spaces between clock marks. Select all the pictures that show 5s in some way."

rich_text.plain_text: "Not quite. Count the fingers on a hand. Look at how tally marks group. Check what a nickel is worth. Count the spaces between clock marks. Select all the pictures that show 5s in some way."

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

text.content: All four images highlight in sequence, showing the 5s connection.

rich_text.plain_text: All four images highlight in sequence, showing the 5s connection.

text.content: "Let’s look at each picture. A hand has 5 fingers. Tally marks bundle by 5s. A nickel equals 5 cents. A clock face marks every 5 minutes. All four use groups of 5. That's what makes counting by 5s so useful. It shows up everywhere. And now you use it for graphs too."

text.content: 2.1  Metacognitive Reflection Two Scales Your  [s2_1_metacognitive_reflection_two_scales_your]

rich_text.plain_text: 2.1  Metacognitive Reflection Two Scales Your  [s2_1_metacognitive_reflection_two_scales_your]

text.content: Simple display showing: "Scale of 1: Each symbol = 1" | "Scale of 2: Each symbol = 2" | “Scale of 5: Each symbol = 5"

rich_text.plain_text: Simple display showing: "Scale of 1: Each symbol = 1" | "Scale of 2: Each symbol = 2" | “Scale of 5: Each symbol = 5"

text.content: "You now

rich_text.plain_text: "You now

text.content: know THREE scales

rich_text.plain_text: know THREE scales

text.content: for making graphs. If you had to show 30 items on a graph, which scale would need fewer symbols?"

rich_text.plain_text: for making graphs. If you had to show 30 items on a graph, which scale would need fewer symbols?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Scale of 1", "Scale of 2", "Scale of 5", "They'd need the same"]
"If you had to show 30 items on a graph, which scale would need fewer symbols?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Scale of 1", "Scale of 2", "Scale of 5", "They'd need the same"]
"If you had to show 30 items on a graph, which scale would need fewer symbols?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected Scale of 5, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected Scale of 5, correct

text.content: "That's right. Scale of 5. You'd need 6 symbols instead of 15 symbols with scale of 2 or 30 symbols with scale of 1. Bigger scale, fewer symbols."

rich_text.plain_text: "That's right. Scale of 5. You'd need 6 symbols instead of 15 symbols with scale of 2 or 30 symbols with scale of 1. Bigger scale, fewer symbols."

text.content: ❌ Student selected Scale of 2, thinking it needs fewer symbols

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected Scale of 2, thinking it needs fewer symbols

text.content: "Not exactly. With Scale of 2, each symbol is 2 items. How many symbols would you need to show 30 items?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Not exactly. With Scale of 2, each symbol is 2 items. How many symbols would you need to show 30 items?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected Scale of 1, thinking it needs fewer symbols

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected Scale of 1, thinking it needs fewer symbols

text.content: "Let’s think about this. With Scale of 1, each symbol is 1 item. How many symbols would you need to show 30 items?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Let’s think about this. With Scale of 1, each symbol is 1 item. How many symbols would you need to show 30 items?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected they'd need the same number of symbols

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected they'd need the same number of symbols

text.content: "Let's compare. Scale of 1 means each symbol stands for 1 item. Scale of 2 means each symbol stands for 2 items. Scale of 5 means each symbol stands for 5 items. They all would need a different number of symbols. Which scale covers more items per symbol?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Let's compare. Scale of 1 means each symbol stands for 1 item. Scale of 2 means each symbol stands for 2 items. Scale of 5 means each symbol stands for 5 items. They all would need a different number of symbols. Which scale covers more items per symbol?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: "Let me show you how to think about this. With Scale of 1, each symbol is 1 item, so 30 items needs 30 symbols. With Scale of 2, each symbol is 2 items, so 30 items needs 15 symbols. With Scale of 5, each symbol is 5 items, so 30 items needs only 6 symbols. The bigger the scale, the fewer symbols you need. Scale of 5 needs the fewest symbols."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you how to think about this. With Scale of 1, each symbol is 1 item, so 30 items needs 30 symbols. With Scale of 2, each symbol is 2 items, so 30 items needs 15 symbols. With Scale of 5, each symbol is 5 items, so 30 items needs only 6 symbols. The bigger the scale, the fewer symbols you need. Scale of 5 needs the fewest symbols."

text.content: ⏭️ Student presses Next

rich_text.plain_text: ⏭️ Student presses Next

text.content: "You're building a toolkit. Scale of 1 works. Scale of 2 works. Scale of 5 works. Soon you'll learn when to use each one."

rich_text.plain_text: "You're building a toolkit. Scale of 1 works. Scale of 2 works. Scale of 5 works. Soon you'll learn when to use each one."

text.content: "You started this unit knowing scale of 1 and 2. Now you can make graphs with scale of 5 too."

rich_text.plain_text: "You started this unit knowing scale of 1 and 2. Now you can make graphs with scale of 5 too."

text.content: "And you made BOTH picture graphs AND bar graphs. Two formats, both showing the same data."

rich_text.plain_text: "And you made BOTH picture graphs AND bar graphs. Two formats, both showing the same data."

text.content: "Three scales in your toolkit. Two graph formats you can create. You're ready for more."

rich_text.plain_text: "Three scales in your toolkit. Two graph formats you can create. You're ready for more."

text.content: "Next time, we'll add scale of 10 to your toolkit."
